heading: Competitive acquisition of outpatient drugs and biologicals
label: 1395w–3b Competitive acquisition of outpatient drugs and biologicals

children.label: i
children.text: competitive acquisition areas are established for contract award purposes for acquisition of and payment for categories of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals (as defined in paragraph (2)) under this part;

children.label: ii
children.text: each physician is given the opportunity annually to elect to obtain drugs and biologicals under the program, rather than under ; andsection 1395w–3a of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: each physician who elects to obtain drugs and biologicals under the program makes an annual selection under paragraph (5) of the contractor through which drugs and biologicals within a category of drugs and biologicals will be acquired and delivered to the physician under this part.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: B Implementation
children.text: For purposes of implementing the program, the Secretary shall establish categories of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals. The Secretary shall phase in the program with respect to those categories beginning in 2006 in such manner as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Waiver of certain provisions
children.label: C Waiver of certain provisions
children.text: In order to promote competition, in carrying out the program the Secretary may waive such provisions of the Federal Acquisition Regulation as are necessary for the efficient implementation of this section, other than provisions relating to confidentiality of information and such other provisions as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: is not likely to result in significant savings; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is likely to have an adverse impact on access to such drugs or biologicals.

children.heading: Competitively biddable drugs and biologicals and program defined
children.label: 2 Competitively biddable drugs and biologicals and program defined

children.heading: Competitively biddable drugs and biologicals defined
children.label: A Competitively biddable drugs and biologicals defined
children.text: The term “competitively biddable drugs and biologicals” means a drug or biological described in section 1395u()(1)(C) of this title and furnished on or after .oJanuary 1, 2006

children.heading: Program
children.label: B Program
children.text: The term “program” means the competitive acquisition program under this section.

children.heading: Competitive acquisition area; area
children.label: C Competitive acquisition area; area
children.text: The terms “competitive acquisition area” and “area” mean an appropriate geographic region established by the Secretary under the program.

children.heading: Contractor
children.label: D Contractor
children.text: The term “contractor” means an entity that has entered into a contract with the Secretary under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the claim for such drugs and biologicals shall be submitted by the contractor that supplied the drugs and biologicals;

children.label: ii
children.text: collection of amounts of any deductible and coinsurance applicable with respect to such drugs and biologicals shall be the responsibility of such contractor and shall not be collected unless the drug or biological is administered to the individual involved; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the payment under this section (and related amounts of any applicable deductible and coinsurance) for such drugs and biologicals shall be made only to such contractor upon receipt of a claim for a drug or biological supplied by the contractor for administration to a beneficiary.

children.heading: Process for adjustments
children.label: B Process for adjustments
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a process for adjustments to payments in the case in which payment is made for drugs and biologicals which were billed at the time of dispensing but which were not actually administered.

children.heading: Information for purposes of cost-sharing
children.label: C Information for purposes of cost-sharing
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a process by which physicians submit information to contractors for purposes of the collection of any applicable deductible or coinsurance amounts under subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Post-payment review process
children.label: D Post-payment review process
children.text: The Secretary shall establish (by program instruction or otherwise) a post-payment review process (which may include the use of statistical sampling) to assure that payment is made for a drug or biological under this section only if the drug or biological has been administered to a beneficiary. The Secretary shall recoup, offset, or collect any overpayments determined by the Secretary under such process.

children.label: A
children.text: the drugs or biologicals are supplied by a contractor with a contract under this section for such category of drugs and biologicals and area; and

children.label: B
children.text: the physician has elected such contractor under paragraph (5) for such category and area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a process for the selection of a contractor, on an annual basis and in such exigent circumstances as the Secretary may provide and with respect to each category of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals for an area by selecting physicians.

children.heading: Timing of selection
children.label: ii Timing of selection
children.text: The selection of a contractor under clause (i) shall be made at the time of the election described in for this section to apply and shall be coordinated with agreements entered into under .section 1395w–3a(a) of this titlesection 1395u(h) of this title

children.heading: Information on contractors
children.label: B Information on contractors
children.text: The Secretary shall make available to physicians on an ongoing basis, through a directory posted on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services or otherwise and upon request, a list of the contractors under this section in the different competitive acquisition areas.

children.heading: Selecting physician defined
children.label: C Selecting physician defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “selecting physician” means, with respect to a contractor and category and competitive acquisition area, a physician who has elected this section to apply and has selected to apply under this section such contractor for such category and area.

children.heading: Contract for competitively biddable drugs and biologicals
children.label: 1 Contract for competitively biddable drugs and biologicals
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a competition among entities for the acquisition of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals. Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, in the case of a multiple source drug, the Secretary shall conduct such competition among entities for the acquisition of at least one competitively biddable drug and biological within each billing and payment code within each category for each competitive acquisition area.

children.heading: Capacity to supply competitively biddable drug or biological within category
children.label: i Capacity to supply competitively biddable drug or biological within category

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The entity has sufficient arrangements to acquire and to deliver competitively biddable drugs and biologicals within such category in the area specified in the contract.

children.heading: Shipment methodology
children.label: II Shipment methodology
children.text: The entity has arrangements in effect for the shipment at least 5 days each week of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals under the contract and for the timely delivery (including for emergency situations) of such drugs and biologicals in the area under the contract.

children.heading: Quality, service, financial performance and solvency standards
children.label: ii Quality, service, financial performance and solvency standards
I
children.text: the establishment of procedures for the prompt response and resolution of complaints of physicians and individuals and of inquiries regarding the shipment of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals; and

children.label: II
children.text: a grievance and appeals process for the resolution of disputes.

children.label: i
children.text: the suspension or revocation, by the Federal Government or a State government, of the entity’s license for the distribution of drugs or biologicals (including controlled substances); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the exclusion of the entity under from participation under this subchapter.section 1320a–7 of this title

children.heading: Application of Medicare Provider Ombudsman
children.label: C Application of Medicare Provider Ombudsman
children.text: For provision providing for a program-wide Medicare Provider Ombudsman to review complaints, see , as added by section 923 of the Medicare Prescription Drug, Improvement, and Modernization Act of 2003.section 1395ee(b) of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Awarding multiple contracts for a category and area
children.label: 3 Awarding multiple contracts for a category and area
A
children.text: The bid prices for competitively biddable drugs and biologicals within the category and area.

children.label: B
children.text: Bid price for distribution of such drugs and biologicals.

children.label: C
children.text: Ability to ensure product integrity.

children.label: D
children.text: Customer service.

children.label: E
children.text: Past experience in the distribution of drugs and biologicals, including controlled substances.

children.label: F
children.text: Such other factors as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A contract entered into with an entity under the competition conducted pursuant to paragraph (1) is subject to terms and conditions that the Secretary may specify consistent with this section.

children.heading: Period of contracts
children.label: B Period of contracts
children.text: A contract under this section shall be for a term of 3 years, but may be terminated by the Secretary or the entity with appropriate, advance notice.

children.heading: Integrity of drug and biological distribution system
children.label: C Integrity of drug and biological distribution system
i
children.text: acquire all drug and biological products it distributes directly from the manufacturer or from a distributor that has acquired the products directly from the manufacturer; and

children.label: ii
children.text: comply with any product integrity safeguards as may be determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Compliance with code of conduct and fraud and abuse rules
children.label: D Compliance with code of conduct and fraud and abuse rules
i
children.text: the contractor shall comply with a code of conduct, specified or recognized by the Secretary, that includes standards relating to conflicts of interest; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the contractor shall comply with all applicable provisions relating to prevention of fraud and abuse, including compliance with applicable guidelines of the Department of Justice and the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Direct delivery of drugs and biologicals to physicians
children.label: E Direct delivery of drugs and biologicals to physicians
i
children.text: require a physician to submit a prescription for each individual treatment; or

children.label: ii
children.text: change a physician’s flexibility in terms of writing a prescription for drugs or biologicals for a single treatment or a course of treatment.

children.heading: Permitting access to drugs and biologicals
children.label: 5 Permitting access to drugs and biologicals
A
children.text: The drugs or biologicals are required immediately.

children.label: B
children.text: The physician could not have reasonably anticipated the immediate requirement for the drugs or biologicals.

children.label: C
children.text: The contractor could not deliver to the physician the drugs or biologicals in a timely manner.

children.label: D
children.text: The drugs or biologicals were administered in an emergency situation.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 6 Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as waiving applicable State requirements relating to licensing of pharmacies.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In awarding a contract for a category of drugs and biologicals in an area under the program, the Secretary shall consider with respect to each entity seeking to be awarded a contract the bid price and the other factors referred to in subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: Bid defined
children.label: 2 Bid defined
children.text: In this section, the term “bid” means an offer to furnish a competitively biddable drug or biological for a particular price and time period.

children.heading: Bidding on a national or regional basis
children.label: 3 Bidding on a national or regional basis
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as precluding a bidder from bidding for contracts in all areas of the United States or as requiring a bidder to submit a bid for all areas of the United States.

children.heading: Uniformity of bids within area
children.label: 4 Uniformity of bids within area
children.text: The amount of the bid submitted under a contract offer for any competitively biddable drug or biological for an area shall be the same for that drug or biological for all portions of that area.

children.label: A
children.text: in that subparagraph to a “manufacturer or wholesaler” is deemed a reference to a “bidder” under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: in that section to “prices charged for drugs” is deemed a reference to a “bid” submitted under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: in clause (i) of that section to “this section”, is deemed a reference to “part B of subchapter XVIII”.

children.label: A
children.text: include all costs related to the delivery of the drug or biological to the selecting physician (or other point of delivery); and

children.label: B
children.text: include the costs of dispensing (including shipping) of such drug or biological and management fees, but shall not include any costs related to the administration of the drug or biological, or wastage, spillage, or spoilage.

children.heading: Price adjustments during contract period; disclosure of costs
children.label: 7 Price adjustments during contract period; disclosure of costs
A
children.text: disclosure to the Secretary the contractor’s reasonable, net acquisition costs for periods specified by the Secretary, not more often than quarterly, of the contract; and

children.label: B
children.text: appropriate price adjustments over the period of the contract to reflect significant increases or decreases in a contractor’s reasonable, net acquisition costs, as so disclosed.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Payment under this section for competitively biddable drugs or biologicals shall be based on bids submitted and accepted under this section for such drugs or biologicals in an area. Based on such bids the Secretary shall determine a single payment amount for each competitively biddable drug or biological in the area.

children.heading: New drugs and biologicals
children.label: A New drugs and biologicals
children.text: A competitively biddable drug or biological for which a payment and billing code has not been established.

children.heading: Other cases
children.label: B Other cases
children.text: Such other exceptional cases as the Secretary may specify in regulations.

children.heading: Application of coinsurance
children.label: 1 Application of coinsurance
children.text: Payment under this section for competitively biddable drugs and biologicals shall be in an amount equal to 80 percent of the payment basis described in subsection (d)(1).

children.heading: Deductible
children.label: 2 Deductible
children.text: Before applying paragraph (1), the individual shall be required to meet the deductible described in section 1395(b) of this title.l

children.heading: Collection
children.label: 3 Collection
children.text: Such coinsurance and deductible shall be collected by the contractor that supplies the drug or biological involved. Subject to subsection (a)(3)(B), such coinsurance and deductible may be collected in a manner similar to the manner in which the coinsurance and deductible are collected for durable medical equipment under this part.

children.heading: Use in exclusion cases
children.label: 1 Use in exclusion cases
children.text: If the Secretary excludes a drug or biological (or class of drugs or biologicals) under subsection (a)(1)(D), the Secretary may provide for payment to be made under this part for such drugs and biologicals (or class) using the payment methodology under .section 1395w–3a of this title

children.heading: Application of requirement for assignment
children.label: 2 Application of requirement for assignment
children.text: For provision requiring assignment of claims for competitively biddable drugs and biologicals, see section 1395u()(3) of this title.o

children.heading: Protection for beneficiary in case of medical necessity denial
children.label: 3 Protection for beneficiary in case of medical necessity denial
children.text: For protection of individuals against liability in the case of medical necessity determinations, see .section 1395u(b)(3)(B)(ii)(III) of this title
the establishment of payment amounts under subsection (d)(1);
the awarding of contracts under this section;
the establishment of competitive acquisition areas under subsection (a)(2)(C);
the phased-in implementation under subsection (a)(1)(B);
the selection of categories of competitively biddable drugs and biologicals for competitive acquisition under such subsection or the selection of a drug in the case of multiple source drugs; or
the bidding structure and number of contractors selected under this section.
